completedWork: H-Bridge Motor Driver Simulation - NEW: Interactive bidirectional DC motor control simulation with comprehensive H-bridge visualization. Features: Motor voltage (1-48V), motor current (0.1-20A), PWM frequency (100Hz-20kHz), duty cycle (0-100%), direction control (Forward/Reverse/Brake/Coast). Real-time calculations: effective voltage (V_eff = V_in × duty_cycle), motor power (P = V_eff × I_avg), power dissipation per MOSFET, regenerative braking energy. Visual feedback: Interactive H-bridge circuit diagram with 4 MOSFETs showing ON/OFF states with color coding, current flow animation, motor rotation animation (speed proportional to duty cycle), PWM waveform visualization, state indicator. Educational content: H-bridge operation theory, truth table for all switching states (Q1-Q4 combinations), shoot-through protection explanation with dead-time delay importance, PWM speed control principles, regenerative braking with energy recovery (30% efficiency), applications (robotics wheel drive, electric vehicles, industrial automation, drones/ESCs, RC vehicles), pro tips (MOSFET selection with low R_DS(on), flyback diodes for inductive kickback, dead-time 100-500ns, gate drivers, dedicated H-bridge ICs like L298N/L293D/DRV8871). Quick presets: Small DC motor (6V, 500mA), Hobby servo (5V, 1A), Rover motor (12V, 5A), Industrial motor (24V, 10A).

Battery State of Charge (SoC) Estimator Calculator - NEW: Comprehensive battery SoC estimation using voltage-based and coulomb counting methods. Features: Battery chemistry selection (Li-Ion, LiPo, Lead-Acid, NiMH) with chemistry-specific discharge curves, rated capacity (100-20000 mAh), current voltage input, load current (0-50A), battery temperature (-10°C to 60°C), initial SoC for coulomb counting. Real-time calculations: Voltage-based SoC using linear interpolation on chemistry-specific curves, Coulomb counting with temperature compensation (SoC = Initial capacity - ∫I dt), Combined SoC (weighted average of voltage and coulomb methods), Temperature compensation factor (optimal 20-25°C = 100%, <0°C = 50%, 10-20°C = 75-100%, >40°C = 90%), Remaining capacity (adjusted for temperature), Remaining energy (Wh), Runtime remaining (based on current load), Internal resistance estimation. Visual feedback: Interactive battery graphic with fill animation and color-coded SoC zones (green >80%, yellow 40-80%, orange 20-40%, red <20%), Voltage vs SoC discharge curve with chemistry-specific data points, current operating point indicator, temperature effect visualization with zones (too cold <10°C, optimal 10-25°C, too hot >40°C), real-time gauge/meter display. Educational content: Battery chemistries comparison (Li-Ion/LiPo: 200-265 Wh/kg, 3.7V nominal, flat curve; Lead-Acid: 30-40 Wh/kg, 12V, heavy; NiMH: lower energy density), SoC estimation methods (voltage-based, coulomb counting, combined/Kalman filters), factors affecting accuracy (load current IR drop, temperature, battery age, chemistry variations, hysteresis), BMS overview (cell balancing, overcharge/over-discharge protection, temperature monitoring, SoH estimation), best practices for battery health (avoid deep discharge, charge to 80% daily, store at 40-60%, use approved chargers, avoid extreme temperatures, don't leave at 100% extended periods), glossary (SoC, SoH, DoD, OCV). Quick presets: 18650 Li-ion cell (3.7V, 2500mAh), LiPo 3S drone (11.1V, 2200mAh), Car battery (12V, 50Ah), AA NiMH (1.2V, 2000mAh).
